description_long: This is a vintage-style gramophone record player with a large, flared brass horn mounted on a wooden base. The base is rectangular and features ornate carved details, including a decorative speaker grille and a retro radio dial on the front. A black vinyl record sits atop a circular platter, with a tonearm and stylus assembly positioned to the side. The horn is metallic and has a smooth, shiny finish, curving upward and outward to amplify sound. The wood base has a warm, polished appearance, and the overall design evokes early 20th-century audio equipment. The object combines both functional and decorative elements, making it a striking piece for display or use.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large brass horn, ornate wooden base, and visible vinyl record and tonearm. The design is both decorative and functional, reminiscent of early 20th-century audio devices.
description_view_0: Front angled view showing the brass horn, vinyl record, tonearm, and detailed radio dial with speaker grille on the wooden base.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the back of the horn, the flat wooden base, and the absence of front-facing controls or dials.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the curve of the horn, the tonearm resting on the platter, and the plain wooden side panel.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the horn's opening visible, the tonearm, and a decorative grille on the wooden base.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present and circular, supporting a black vinyl record. It appears to be made of plastic or metal, with a smooth, matte black finish and no visible grooves or patterns. The platter is proportionally smaller than the wooden base but large enough to accommodate a standard record.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, black in color, and appears to be made of plastic or metal. It is straight, of moderate length, and is positioned resting on the edge of the platter. The tonearm includes a visible joint near its base for pivoting.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is not clearly visible, but its presence is implied at the end of the tonearm where it meets the record.
attribute_control_knobs: There is a single prominent control knob located on the front of the wooden base, below the radio dial. It is round, metallic, and silver in color, with a smooth finish and no visible labels or markings.
